title: Factors of production, impact of human capital on production, and the role of entrepreneurs
description: Practice understanding of factors of production, impact of human capital on production, and the role of entrepreneurs
topic_path: Factors of production, impact of human capital on production, and the role of entrepreneurs

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these best defines *land* as economic factor of production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pieces of the earth that people buy in order to build on
all natural resources
pieces of the earth that are reserved for growing crops
the entire surface of the earth

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these best define *capital* as an economic factor of production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: man-made resources that are used to produce goods and services
money
natural resources that are used to produce goods and services
investment

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the field of economics, which of the following best defines the term 'entrepreneur'?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An individual who solely consumes goods and services produced by businesses.
An individual who starts and manages a business, assuming the risks and rewards associated with it.
An individual who is responsible for enforcing the business laws in a country.
An individual who is employed by a company and receives a fixed monthly salary.

assessment_items.question_content:
In economics, the factors of production are the basic building blocks of all goods and services. 

**Which of the following scenarios best demonstrates the effective use of all four factors of production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A farmer uses a new irrigation system to increase crop yield on their farmland.
A factory hires additional workers to operate new machinery in the production process.
An entrepreneur starts a logging company, purchasing forested land, hiring loggers, and investing in logging equipment.
A software company invests in new computers for their programmers to develop innovative applications.

assessment_items.question_content:
In economics, the factors of production are the basic building blocks of all goods and services. 

**Which example shows all four factors of production ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Bob just got hired as a manager at a local used car lot. He is currently hiring more sales people and adding a car wash to the facility.
An existing company specializing in accessibility equipment is purchasing new vans and a storage lot.
Jill is starting  her own printing business. She has leased a store front, hired an apprentice, and purchased printing equipment.
A mobile tutoring service hired high school students to teach elementary students math. They also give each new employee a new company shirt and a special calculator.

assessment_items.question_content:
In economics, the factors of production are the basic building blocks of all goods and services. 

**Of the following choices, which one includes all four factors of production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Marcy just purchased land and is using the new lot to construct a building that will manufacturer bicycles. She has hired a contractor to construct the building and has equipment on order that will aid her in assembling the bikes at this new company.
Trevon just founded a clothing company that specializes in soccer attire. He will be employing a staff of three people.
The jewelry company in the mall just leased the area next to their current location. With this expansion, they are also buying more equipment.
Susie, the founder and owner of a dog tag making business, is looking to expand her business. She has bought a new dog tag making machine and has hired more staff.

assessment_items.question_content:
Human capital is an important factor of production in economics.

**Which of the following actions by a company would most effectively expand its human capital and improve long-term productivity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Investing in state-of-the-art machinery to automate production processes.
Providing employees with ongoing training and opportunities to earn advanced certifications in their field.
Increasing the number of employees to handle a temporary surge in customer demand.
Renovating the company's office space to create a more comfortable working environment.

assessment_items.question_content:
John is a junior software developer at XYZ Company. He recently completed an advanced programming languages training program provided by his employer.

**How did this training program impact John's human capital?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It reduced John's human capital by taking him away from his routine tasks.
There was no effect on John's human capital because it was provided by his employer and not an educational institution.
The training program increased John's human capital by enhancing his knowledge and skills, making him more valuable to his employer.
There was a decrease in John's human capital because he spent time learning instead of contributing to the company.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lina is about to graduate high school and is considering these options:

Choice | Description | Benefits
-| - | -
Community college for Nursing | Associate degree in nursing | Specialized education, high demand, high-paying field
Technical program for Electrician | Certified electrician training | Hands-on training, immediate entry, high earning potential
Minimum wage job post high school | Save money for future education | Work experience, financial stability, savings
Work experience in entry-level jobs | No further education, focus on work | Explore career paths, diverse skills, immediate income

**Which choice is most likely to expand her human capital and increase her income potential in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: community college
technical program
working a minimum wage job
entry-level positions

assessment_items.question_content:
Entrepreneurship is one of the four factors of production in economics. 

**Which of the following scenarios best demonstrates the economic concept of entrepreneurship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A software engineer develops a new app and launches a startup to market and sell it.
A factory owner purchases new equipment to increase production efficiency and reduce costs.
A retail store hires additional staff to handle increased customer demand during the busy season.
A farmer uses improved irrigation techniques to enhance crop yields on their existing farmland.

assessment_items.question_content:
Entrepreneurship is one of the four factors of production in economics. 

**Which answer best represents the economic concept of entrepreneurship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Jill has developed a new leak-free water bottle that she is bringing to market. She is taking out loans for the equipment needed for production and filing a patent for her design.
Jenny, a manager, is hiring more people at the busy restaurant.
Alex is leasing more space so the company he works for can grow.
A new employee has an idea to make the shoe business they work for more profitable.

assessment_items.question_content:
Entrepreneurship is one of the four factors of production in economics. 

**The economic concept of entrepreneurship is shown in which answer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Monique works for a delivery company and tells her boss about a vacant lot seen on her route. She  knows her boss is looking for more space to store delivery trucks.
Sandra asks her boss for a raise and gives her a few reasons she deserves more money.

Brian, an artist, is starting skateboard company that specializes in wild graphics. He is updating his portfolio of art work, coming up with budgets based on the cost of skateboard production, and seeking out funders for this new business.
The manager at the local guitar store has been tasked with buying more equipment in order to limit the amount of human labor needed to run the storefront.
